title: hopea latifolia
intro: Hopea latifolia, also known as the forest ironwood, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Hopea latifolia include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders, making it a valuable plant for promoting overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Hopea latifolia include extracts and infusions that can be used to support cardiovascular health and immunity.
